description_long: This gatehouse is a stone structure with two prominent towers, each topped with steep, gray slate roofs. The taller tower features a wooden lookout platform supported by timber beams, giving it a fortified appearance. The walls are constructed from rough-hewn stone blocks, and the roof is finished with overlapping shingles. Black metal pipes protrude from the towers, possibly serving as chimneys or ventilation ducts. The main building has a rectangular footprint with a small, arched entryway at the base. The overall design is reminiscent of medieval European architecture, emphasizing security and surveillance.
description: A stone gatehouse with two steep-roofed towers, a wooden lookout platform, and black metal chimneys, evoking a fortified medieval style.
description_view_0: View 0 shows the two towers with steep slate roofs, the wooden lookout platform atop the taller tower, and multiple black metal pipes on the roof.
description_view_1: View 1 highlights the main entryway at the base, the stonework of the walls, and the alignment of the towers with the lookout platform clearly visible.
description_view_2: View 2 provides a side perspective, emphasizing the height of the towers, the stone construction, and the arrangement of the chimneys.
description_view_3: View 3 focuses on the rear of the gatehouse, showing the back of the towers, the wooden platform, and the absence of an entryway on this side.
attribute_entryway_design: Small rectangular stone entryway with a single wooden door.
attribute_window_placement: No visible windows; solid stone walls dominate the structure.
